sourceMovie.title: Click
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Click works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Family Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Click clicked for you, The Family Man (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Click?
faq.answer: Try The Family Man, A Man Called Otto, The Time Traveler's Wife—each shares DNA with Click while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Click?
faq.answer: Click (2006) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Click worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
